# ECOS Medicare Solutions — Georgia (full reference)

Website: https://georgiamedicareenrollment.com/
Phone: (770) 285-5174
Service area: State of Georgia (statewide — by phone, video, and locally)

## About
ECOS Medicare Solutions is an independent Medicare insurance agency helping Georgia retirees and people
approaching 65 compare their Medicare options clearly, patiently, and at no cost. Because independent agents
are paid by the insurance carriers when a client enrolls, there is no charge to the consumer, and plan premiums
are the same whether you enroll with our help or on your own.

## Agent / author
Darin Weidauer, MBA, RSSA — independent Medicare insurance agent, credentialed gerontologist (since 2014),
Registered Social Security Analyst, and 22-year U.S. Air Force veteran (retired officer). NPN 18580338.
Author of "Retire With Confidence: The Medicare Guide." Former Professor of Aerospace Studies at Loyola
Marymount University; has lectured at more than 50 colleges and universities.

## How Medicare fits together
- Part A: inpatient hospital, skilled nursing, hospice (premium-free for most).
- Part B: doctors, outpatient, preventive care. 2026 premium $202.90/month; deductible $283.
- Part C (Medicare Advantage): a private all-in-one plan bundling A, B, and usually Part D, often $0 premium,
  using a local network. Includes specialized plans: C-SNP (chronic conditions), I-SNP (institutional/facility
  care), and D-SNP (for people with both Medicare and Medicaid).
- Part D: prescription drug coverage. 2026 out-of-pocket cap $2,100; max deductible $615; national base premium $38.99.
- Medicare Supplement (Medigap): works with Original Medicare, usable with any U.S. provider that accepts Medicare,
  standardized by plan letter; pair with a standalone Part D plan.

## 2026 IRMAA (income-related surcharge), based on 2024 MAGI
Single / Joint thresholds and 2026 Part B total premium (Part D surcharge added to plan premium):
- <=$109,000 / <=$218,000: Part B $202.90, Part D +$0.00
- $109,001-$137,000 / $218,001-$274,000: Part B $284.10, Part D +$14.50
- $137,001-$171,000 / $274,001-$342,000: Part B $405.80, Part D +$37.50
- $171,001-$205,000 / $342,001-$410,000: Part B $527.50, Part D +$60.40
- $205,001-$499,999 / $410,001-$749,999: Part B $649.20, Part D +$83.30
- >=$500,000 / >=$750,000: Part B $689.90, Part D +$91.00

## Veterans (TRICARE For Life and VA)
- TRICARE For Life requires Medicare Part A and B; pays secondary to Medicare; its pharmacy is creditable, so a
  separate Part D plan is usually unnecessary. An MA-only plan can add dental/vision without duplicating drug coverage.
- VA health care does not coordinate with Medicare; VA medical is NOT creditable for Part B (enroll on time to avoid a
  lifelong penalty), but VA pharmacy IS creditable for Part D. Having both gives more options.

## Medicare + Georgia Medicaid (dual eligible)
Medicare pays first; Georgia Medicaid (administered by the Department of Community Health; eligibility via the
Division of Family & Children Services) may help with cost-sharing. Medicare Savings Programs (QMB/SLMB/QI) help with
the Part B premium; Extra Help lowers Part D costs; D-SNPs coordinate both. Free state counseling: GeorgiaCares
(Georgia's SHIP), 1-800-669-8387.

## Areas served
Metros: Atlanta, Augusta, Columbus, Savannah, Macon, Athens, Gainesville, Warner Robins, Albany, Valdosta,
Brunswick, Rome, Dalton. Regions: North Georgia & the Mountains, Metro Atlanta, Middle Georgia, the CSRA,
the Chattahoochee Valley, Coastal Georgia & the Golden Isles, Southwest Georgia.

## Common questions
- Cost to work with us: none; agents are paid by carriers at enrollment.
- Enrollment windows: Initial Enrollment Period (7 months around 65th birthday); Annual Election Period Oct 15–Dec 7;
  Medicare Advantage Open Enrollment Jan 1–Mar 31; Special Enrollment Periods after qualifying events.
- Advantage vs Medigap: depends on your doctors, prescriptions, travel, and budget.

## Compliance
We do not offer every plan available in your area. We represent 8 organizations which offer 87 products in Georgia. Please contact Medicare.gov, 1-800-MEDICARE, or your local
State Health Insurance Program (SHIP) to get information on all your options. ECOS Medicare Solutions is not
connected with or endorsed by the U.S. government or the federal Medicare program, and is not affiliated with the
VA, the Department of Defense, or TRICARE. This is a solicitation for insurance; a licensed agent may contact you.
